mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals severe labor market deterioration, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer durables demand, which forces equity markets to price in lower near-term cash flows, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained aggregate supply and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation exceeds 0.4%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases the cost of capital and forces a downward repricing of equities, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 65% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of contracting real GDP growth and rising federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that severely dampens equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-FEDFUNDS interaction term fails to significantly improve return prediction accuracy over a univariate model (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain capacity, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.
